title: The One Muscle Car That Quietly Outran Everything In Its Era - HotCars
content:
Angel Sergeev is a seasoned automotive journalist with over 15 years of experience covering the automotive industry. Born in Sofia, Bulgaria, he began his writing career in 2010 while pursuing a degree in Transportation Engineering.

His early work included contributions to the local edition of F1 Racing magazine (now GP Racing magazine) and roles at various automotive websites and magazines. In 2013, Angel joined Motor1.com (formerly WorldCarFans), where he dedicated over a decade to delivering daily news and feature articles. His expertise spans a wide range of topics, including electric vehicles, classic cars, and industry topics. Angel's commitment to automotive journalism is further demonstrated by his membership in the Bulgarian Car of the Year jury since 2013. The golden age of     muscle cars looked like a street fight dressed up as a marketing war. Chevrolet had the Chevelle,     Plymouth had the Hemi ’Cuda, Pontiac had The Judge. Every brand pushed cubes, stripes, hood scoops, and horsepower numbers that sounded large enough to bend the calendar. In 1970, GM even dropped its over-400-cubic-inch limit for midsize cars, and the whole segment got louder, brasher, and a lot more serious in a hurry. However, the quickest car of the bunch, at least in period magazine testing, was not the one most people still brag about at every cars-and-coffee parking lot summit. One overlooked machine clicked off a 13.38-second quarter-mile at 105.5 mph, edging better-known heavy hitters like the Hemi ’Cuda and the LS6 Chevelle in comparable period tests. It embarrassed the legends, then hid in plain sight. Buick once released a model that was so powerful that GM downplayed its true output to dodge corporate limits and keep insurers off its back. Ask a room full of muscle car fans to name the kings of 1970, and the same few cars usually stomp into the conversation first. The Hemi cars show up fast, so do the LS6 Chevelles and the wildest GTOs. That makes sense, of course – those cars had the poster power, the louder ad copy, and the kind of styling that looked fast even while parked. They owned the reputation game before the green light even dropped. But memory cheats. It hangs onto stripes, wings, and folklore, and tends to forget the cars that simply worked. The     quarter-mile, on the other hand, acts like a rude lie detector – it does not care who got the bigger magazine cover or whose hood decal shouted the loudest. It only cares who got to the traps first. The other problem sits in the way the era sold power. Bench racers loved horsepower because the number looked clean and easy. A 425-hp Hemi or a 450-hp LS6 could win an argument before the engine ever fired, yet street performance rarely followed a neat ad-man script. Torque, axle ratio, traction, weight, and shift timing mattered just as much, sometimes more. Then there was brand baggage. Some divisions sold rebellion, others sold comfort, quiet, and a ride soft enough to keep coffee in the cup. The sleeper in this story came from the second camp, and period testers even played with that image, calling it an “old man’s car” while watching it rip off brutally quick numbers. Any car that could smoke a Chevelle SS 454 LS6 deserves to be remembered more than the Buick GS 455. Once the time slips hit the table, the story gets awkward for the usual kings. Motor Trend tested that car at 13.38 seconds and 105.5 mph in the quarter. In a period Cars road test, the 1970 Hemi ’Cuda ran 13.45 at 105 mph, while Hot Rod’s 1970 LS6 Chevelle test posted a 13.44 at 108.7 mph. Motor Trend's Ram Air IV GTO test ran 13.87 seconds in the quarter-mile. All of them were serious machines, but one was still quicker than the rest of that group on paper. The real sting comes from the context. The quick car in question did not arrive as some stripped drag special. Motor Trend weighed the automatic version at 3,810 pounds and the four-speed at just under 3,750. It had a full interior, real street manners, and the kind of build quality the magazine praised as strong and solid. It could idle through traffic, then knock out a 13-second pass. That made it more dangerous than a one-trick bruiser. Its advertised horsepower also looked almost polite next to the brawlers of the day. The engine carried a factory rating of 360 horsepower, which sounded modest beside 425 from the Hemi and 450 from the     LS6. Yet its torque figure sat at 510 pound-feet, and it peaked at just 2,800 rpm. That kind of low-speed twist could move a heavy A-body in a hurry. Then came the sneaky part. Motor Trend found that the automatic version actually got through the quarter quicker than the manual. Buick had recalibrated the automatic’s governor to allow higher-rpm shifts, and the magazine said the automatic shifted perfectly at 6,000 rpm. The stick car ran well too, but it had a habit of bogging near 5,500. That car was the 1970     Buick GS 455 Stage 1, and the plain GS matters every bit as much as the louder     GSX cousin. This was the Buick that slipped past the noise and landed the punch. Motor Trend’s 13.38-second run at 105.5 mph led Hot Rod, years later, to note that the magazine had proclaimed the Stage 1 the quickest American production vehicle it had tested at the time. That line still sounds absurd until the numbers show up and back it. Then it sounds absurd again. The hardware explains why it hit so hard. Buick's 455-cubic-inch V8 used a 4.3125-inch bore and 3.90-inch stroke, 10.5:1 compression in Stage 1 trim, a Rochester Quadrajet four-barrel, and a factory rating of 360 horsepower at 4,600 rpm with 510 pound-feet at 2,800 rpm.     Stage 1 brought larger valves, relieved valve pockets in the heads, stronger springs, a hotter cam with more lift and duration, richer carb jetting, more ignition advance, and a low-restriction dual exhaust.     Buick basically hid a fist inside a driving glove. The rest of the package looked just as smart. Buyers could pair the engine with a Turbo Hydra-Matic 400 automatic or a close-ratio four-speed. Stage 1 cars used a 3.64:1 performance axle in most cases, with a 3.42 available when air conditioning entered the picture. The automatic got specific internal tweaks for firmer shifts. Even the optional Rallye Ride package came cheap and added the kind of suspension help that made the big Buick more than a straight-line hammer. At the time, Buick charged $199.05 for the Stage 1 option on the GS 455. The wild GSX package could add the stripes, spoilers, hood tach, and billboard tires, but the regular GS 455 Stage 1 kept the same heart with far less theater. That subtlety made it the perfect sleeper. First, Buick’s image got in its own way. The division built comfort cars and upscale cruisers. The brand’s dealers were more used to selling Electra 225s than explaining a street racer’s revolution on the showroom floor. In other words, the car arrived faster than the brand’s reputation could keep up. That happens all the time in performance history. Second, the flashy version stole some of the memory. The GSX, with its stripes, wing, hood tach, and two wild colors, looked like the kind of thing that begged for a magazine centerfold. But the standard GS 455 Stage 1 did most of its work without all that costume jewelry. Production numbers show 2,465 Stage 1 hardtops and 232 Stage 1 convertibles for 1970, while the GSX Stage 1 accounted for just 400 hardtops. Third, the Buick lost the bench-racing argument before the race started. A 360-hp rating looked tame parked next to 425 and 450 on a showroom window sticker. Most people saw the number and stopped thinking – they missed the 510 pound-feet torque peak at 2,800 rpm, the careful transmission tuning, and the way the car actually behaved on a drag strip. The GS 455 Stage 1 did not win the beauty contest or the shouting match, but it won the part that mattered. That is why the 1970 Buick GS 455 Stage 1 still matters today. It proved that a muscle car did not need comic-book styling or a famous nickname to become a killer – it could carry a softer image, better manners, and a quieter cabin, then go out and run numbers that forced the whole room to recalculate. The formula was a rare one – a blend of abundant torque, strong power, and luxury. Buick built a luxury bruiser long before the phrase turned trendy. The market has started to catch on, though it still has not gone fully insane. Hagerty currently pegs a 1970 GS 455 Stage I sport coupe in good condition at about $50,300, while a Stage I convertible in good condition sits at about $103,000. Those numbers make a strong case for the Buick as one of the more interesting buys in serious muscle. The car offers real period performance, real rarity, and a story that still surprises people who think they already know 1970 by heart. The ceiling climbs far higher when the right car shows up. Hagerty says the highest public sale of a 1970 Buick GS 455 Stage I over the last three years reached $313,500. This is huge, and basically means collectors no longer treat these cars like background music. Not every Stage 1 will land in that zip code, of course, but the old “underrated Buick” line keeps getting harder to say with a straight face when six-figure bids start flying. In the end, the GS 455 Stage 1 did more than run quick. It exposed a blind spot in the whole muscle car myth – the era did not belong only to the loudest names or the flashiest sheetmetal, at least when it came to actual quarter-mile performance. It also belonged to one brutally smart Buick that used torque, gearing, and careful engineering to outrun its own reputation. We want to hear from you! Share your opinions in the thread below and remember to keep it respectful.

title: Setter Ahn Hye-jin, the winner of GS Caltex's championship match, is expected to be disciplined by t.. - 매일경제
content:
Setter Ahn Hye-jin, the winner of GS Caltex's championship match, is expected to be disciplined by the Korea Volleyball Federation (KOVO) for drunk driving. In addition, the national team was excluded from the call-up because it was included in the reason for disqualification. Ahn Hye-jin was booked by police for drunk driving on the 16th and was investigated. Afterwards, he voluntarily reported the fact to his team, and GS Caltex said on social media on the 17th, a day later, "Drunk driving is an act that cannot be tolerated for any reason. I feel a heavy responsibility after confirming the fact that the player is driving under the influence of alcohol," he posted an apology. Ahn Hye-jin also posted a handwritten apology on her SNS. He said, "I deeply apologize for causing a stir by driving under the influence of alcohol. It was entirely my fault, and it was a rash act without any excuse. I will look back on myself with this incident and live with a lifelong reflection so that the same mistakes will not be repeated," he said. GS Caltex reportedly notified KOVO immediately after being informed of the drunk driving by Ahn Hye-jin. A club official said, "I'm sorry for causing concern," and added, "I have nothing to say at the moment. We are continuing our internal investigation," he said. KOVO is also moving after receiving the report on the incident. Early next week, a reward and punishment committee will be held to discuss and decide the level of disciplinary action against Ahn Hye-jin. There have been no cases in professional volleyball that have been disciplined only for drunk driving. It is expected that comprehensive requirements such as internal regulations and personal vocation will be judged by referring to cases of other stocks. In Article 10 (1) of the KOVO Reward and Punishment Regulations, disciplinary action will be taken through the (penalty) committee, such as conciliation, intimidation, ridicule and other inappropriate responses to victims and officials after obtaining membership of the federation and club in connection with sexual crimes, violence, drunk driving, illegal drugs, gambling, match manipulation, racism, past school violence, and human rights violations. In this case, you can receive up to the maximum expulsion from the warning. The penalty will be more than 5 million won. Ahn Hye-jin was also excluded from the women's national volleyball team call-up. He was on the 18-member call-up list and was set to train for Jincheon Training Center in Chungbuk on the 20th. However, it was included in Article 13(9) of the Korean Volleyball Association's articles of association and regulations "National Team Selection and Operation Regulations" and was subject to disqualification. Article (a) stipulates that a person who has been sentenced to a fine of more than KRW 5 million is disqualified less than three years after the sentence is confirmed, and (b) a person who has been sentenced to a fine of less than KRW 5 million and less than two years after the sentence is confirmed. Ahn Hye-jin earned her free agent status after leading GS Caltex to the championship this season. I drew a lot of attention to my next move, but I had to bow down because of the unpleasant thing. ※ This article was translated using AI technology for reader convenience. title: Gen.G's 'Canyon': "The Game Flow Felt Unnatural" - Inven Global
content: Gen.G fell to Hanwha Life Esports in a 0-2 sweep, bringing their record to 3-3. Given that Gen.G finished with a 29-1 record last year, a three-loss tally this early feels quite unfamiliar. After the match, head coach Yoo Sang-wook assessed the performance as "a listless defeat; we set the wrong direction," while 'Canyon' remarked, "The game flow didn't feel natural." Below is the post-match interview with Gen.G head coach Yoo Sang-wook and 'Canyon' Kim Geon-bu. Q. What is your overall assessment of today's match? Head Coach Yoo Sang-wook= It's disappointing to have lost so listlessly. I think we set the wrong direction. 'Canyon'= It's a shame we lacked flexibility as a team. We will prepare better for the next one. Q. Given how well you performed last year, three losses already feels strange. Head Coach Yoo Sang-wook= We played too rigidly. We were too focused on objectives, and our communication was lacking. Also, we need to provide feedback on our team compositions, which were at a disadvantage in teamfights even when we held the initiative. 'Canyon'= While playing, the flow didn't feel natural. We need to keep talking to each other in-game to keep things connected, but that hasn't been working well. Head Coach Yoo Sang-wook= There was a lot we could have done, but we fell for the opponent's traps and couldn't execute our own game plan. Q. How did you prepare for the match against Hanwha Life? Head Coach Yoo Sang-wook= We prioritized gaining the initiative. However, once we secured it, we failed to play organically. Q. You've been banning Karma frequently. Why is that? Head Coach Yoo Sang-wook= There are several reasons. Sometimes our prepared pick-ban strategy makes a first pick unfavorable, and we have actually left it open in the past as well. Head Coach Yoo Sang-wook= Our performance was disappointing, and our preparation was lacking as well. We will make sure to show a different side of ourselves in the next match. 'Canyon'= I also feel my personal performance was lacking, so I will work hard to improve. title: Goldman Sachs Enters Bitcoin ETFs With Income Focused Crypto Offering - simplywall.st
content:
For a bank known primarily for investment banking, trading, and asset management, this ETF filing represents a clear broadening of Goldman Sachs' approach to digital assets. Crypto related products are moving further into the toolkit of large financial institutions, and ETFs have become a key access point for investors who prefer regulated structures over direct token ownership. The focus on premium income sets this fund apart from simple spot Bitcoin exposure. For investors watching NYSE:GS, this step may indicate how large banks intend to package crypto exposure for clients who want both potential Bitcoin participation and an income component. The filing also places Goldman in more direct competition with peers that already offer Bitcoin ETFs, and it raises questions about how fee levels, liquidity, and distribution will evolve as more income oriented crypto products come to market. title: All About Karol G’s Coachella Shoes: Custom Silver Reeboks and More Footwear Styles - WWD
content: Karol G made history as the first Latina headliner of the festival. Karol G made history at this year’s Coachella Valley Music & Arts Festival as the first Latina headliner, performing a vibrant set on Sunday during weekend one in Indio, Calif. The Colombian musician brought the heat both musically and sartorially, with stage-ready looks that were striking from head to toe, including shoes ranging from sneakers to boots. To align with her bold performance, the recently announced Reebok global ambassador performed in a custom silver version of the athletic brand’s Freestyle Hi sneaker. Bringing back 1980s fitness style, the high-top sneakers feature a puffy ribbed collar and double ankle straps in addition to laces. Her throwback sneakers matched a silver leather bralette and miniskirt set with jagged edges; Brett Alan Nelson came up with the concept, and Michael Schmidt Studios created the look. The singer-songwriter’s multiyear partnership with Reebok was revealed back in January, with Karol G announcing her new role in a TikTok post that generated $1.1 million in Media Impact Value (MIV) in 48 hours. As part of the partnership, she is helping to reintroduce the brand’s Classics Collection. The Reebok representation on stage didn’t end with Karol G, as her background dancers and band showcased a curated blend of iconic Reebok styles, including the Club C 85 (a style worn by Taylor Swift in her “Opalite” music video), Workout Plus, Freestyle Lo, Club C Ground UK and R400. For another on-stage look, Karol G wore custom knee-high boots by Shoe Lab Doheny. Elevated by a stiletto heel, the shoes featured bold blue lace with colorful beading, making the style more complex. The boots accompanied a custom Etro look including a miniskirt with colorful tassels, intricately beaded pieces, a belted corset and a cropped jacket. This look also included a headpiece from Luar. An additional performance look from Karol G featured white boots from Courrèges featuring a form-fitting knee-high shaft and a block heel. The shoes accompanied a white top with yellow, blue and red ruffled sleeves, tributing the Colombian flag, designed by Adan Terriquez. Her white high-low skirt was made by Doni Nahmias. title: GS Caltex's Ahn Hye-jin Apologizes for Drunk Driving - 조선일보
content: KOVO to Decide Disciplinary Actions Ranging from Warning to Expulsion [OSEN=Hong Jisu Reporter] Professional volleyball setter Ahn Hye-jin of GS Caltex, who was caught driving under the influence, posted an apology statement. The GS Caltex volleyball team announced on the 17th, "We have confirmed the fact of Ahn Hye-jin's drunk driving and inform our fans with a heavy sense of responsibility," adding, "Drunk driving is an act that cannot be tolerated under any circumstances. The team expresses deep regret for causing concern over this matter and apologizes sincerely to all fans who support our volleyball team and the V-League." Ahn Hye-jin was reportedly caught by police driving under the influence on the morning of the 16th. She led the team to victory in the 2025-2026 season and had even obtained FA eligibility, but her drunk driving incident has erased her hard-earned achievements in an instant. Ahn also posted a handwritten apology on her SNS. She wrote, "This is Ahn Hye-jin," and acknowledged, "I deeply apologize for causing controversy through drunk driving. This incident was entirely my fault, and there is no room for excuses—it was a reckless act." She continued, "I am sincerely sorry for causing great disappointment and pain to fans who support me, everyone who loves volleyball, and officials from the team and league." GS Caltex immediately reported the incident to the Korea Volleyball Federation (KOVO) upon confirmation. KOVO stated, "Disciplinary actions ranging from a minimum warning and fines to possible expulsion are possible," adding, "We plan to finalize the schedule for the disciplinary committee soon." GS Caltex said, "We have requested cooperation to proceed with procedures according to V-League regulations," and added, "We will also thoroughly investigate the circumstances of this incident and take corresponding measures. Furthermore, to prevent recurrence, we will review and improve the education and management systems for players and staff. We once again deeply apologize for causing concern and disappointment to our fans," through an official SNS apology. Ahn Hye-jin, the winning setter of GS Caltex in the 2025-2026 season, became a free agent (FA) after the 2025-2026 season, but this drunk driving incident will significantly impact her future career. Her national team eligibility could also be revoked. Ahn was named to the Korea Volleyball Association’s national team list announced on the 16th, but under current rules, individuals who receive a fine of 5 million Korean won or more and whose sentences are finalized within three years cannot become national team members. Ahn stated, "I will reflect deeply on this incident and live with a mindset of lifelong repentance to ensure such a mistake is never repeated. I sincerely apologize once again."

Although media reports or rumors about a significant change in a company's business prospects usually cause its stock to trend and lead to an immediate price change, there are always certain fundamental factors that ultimately drive the buy-and-hold decision. Rather than focusing on anything else, we at Zacks prioritize evaluating the change in a company's earnings projection. This is because we believe the fair value for its stock is determined by the present value of its future stream of earnings. Our analysis is essentially based on how sell-side analysts covering the stock are revising their earnings estimates to take the latest business trends into account. When earnings estimates for a company go up, the fair value for its stock goes up as well. And when a stock's fair value is higher than its current market price, investors tend to buy the stock, resulting in its price moving upward. Because of this, empirical studies indicate a strong correlation between trends in earnings estimate revisions and short-term stock price movements. content: To stream KENS 5 on your phone, you need the KENS 5 app. ORIOLES POSTCAST: Baltimore Orioles' bats STUMPED by Gavin Williams, Cleveland Guardians ORIOLES POSTCAST: Baltimore Orioles' bats STUMPED by Gavin Williams, Cleveland Guardians Jayhawks Portal Target? Allen Graves is THE BEST Player in the Portal But Would it Work at Kansas? GIANTS POSTCAST: EXTRA-INNINGS WIN Gives Giants THREE-GAME WIN STREAK SUNDAY OT: What position would maximally improve the Packers if they NAIL the pick at 52? TIGERS POSTCAST: Road Losing Streak SNAPPED! Detroit Tigers BEAT Boston Red Sox, Tarik Skubal 10 K’s SAN ANTONIO — Just days ago, Melissa Rojas was in California representing San Antonio on a bigger stage than ever before. The owner of “Chasing Camila,” a brand named after her daughter, had been invited by Karol G’s team to take part in a Coachella-related market in Palm Desert. It was the kind of opportunity many small business owners work years to reach. “It was just an amazing experience to be able to take my product and showcase them over there,” Rojas said. Her designs, known for their bold colors and cultural influence, were connecting with new customers, and for a moment, everything felt like it was lining up. On her way back to Texas, Rojas got unexpected news: the retail space she relied on in San Antonio had suddenly and permanently closed. “As soon as I got on my flight, I got the word that my physical space was abruptly and permanently closed,” she said. Fiesta season is one of the busiest, and most profitable, times of the year for local vendors. For Rojas, it’s when months of preparation typically turn into a major portion of her income. “I felt like I didn’t get a chance to kind of enjoy it. I kind of felt like I was brought down back to reality,” she said. Rojas says she still doesn’t have clear answers about what happens next, especially when it comes to lost sales and inventory already in the store before it shut down. “I don’t even know what the answer is to anything,” she said. With little time to react, fellow vendors and friends stepped in, packing up her merchandise before she even landed back in San Antonio. Now, instead of relying on a storefront, Rojas is shifting her entire business model, at least for now. “So I have pop-ups every day... I don’t think I’ve had a moment to process what I’m going to do next,” she said. Pop-ups across the city, temporary setups, and social media updates have become her main way of staying afloat during one of the most important sales seasons of the year. Rojas, a single mother, says the loss of a physical space isn’t just about convenience, it’s about stability. It was a place where customers could shop her designs in person, even when she was traveling or juggling other responsibilities. Now, she’s navigating what comes next in real time. “I think this is the time to rely on each other and just kind of ask for help if you need it,” she said. For now, that support from customers, fellow vendors, and the San Antonio community is what’s keeping “Chasing Camila” moving forward. Even as the future of her business remains uncertain, one thing hasn’t changed: the name behind it. A brand built for her daughter, now being carried forward, one pop-up at a time. title: I Walked 17,745 Steps at Coachella in the Same Shoe Karol G Performed in to Put Them to the Test - cosmopolitan.com
content: One headliner and one fashion editor agree the key to surviving Coachella is the right shoe. Coachella has already served up special moments: Justin Bieber singing the YouTube hits that made him, Seventeen cover star Sombr's debut performance, Addison Rae bringing out Maddie Ziegler to dance “Aquamarine” together. And on Sunday night of weekend one, Karol G took the main stage as the first Latina artist to headline Coachella in the festival's 27-year history. As a fashion editor, I couldn’t help but notice her sneakers. Getting ready for the festival myself, I toggled between two shoe options: leather boots or white Reebok Classics. The sneakers won out because my feet already tingled in anticipation of the day ahead, plus they made for an unfussy pair to a sequin top, ripped jeans, and pink socks fashioned as leg warmers. I sent a picture to a friend and received a "hot stuff" response in return. Exactly the validation I needed to walk out the door! The Coachella crowd largely agreed with the instinct toward easy dressing. Festival veteran Kendall Jenner (spotted this year making out with Jacob Elordi) wore a simple white tank top and shorts. By day three, Alix Earle opted for a sweatshirt and the freedom of reckless abandon. Lived-in style references from the aughts recirculated, like Kate Moss at Glastonbury in 2003 in only a vest, micro shorts, studded belt, and mud-clad Hunter boots. My Coachella group chat pings with a relic from 2007 of Vanessa Hudgens and Zac Efron followed by, "Hey so this is the moodboard." The white sneaker fits squarely into this conversation, and it also makes the most practical sense for festival-goers walking the dusty, sun-drenched 1.5-miles from the entrance to the stages (or even me running to Addison Rae after missing the opening song from the bathroom line). By the time I hit 17,745 steps in my own Reeboks, Karol G was taking the stage. The set was elaborate and cavelike, featuring boulders, staircases, metallic outfits, and an all-female mariachi band in royal blue suits. A water sequence with her dancers, choreographed by Parris Goebel, was one of the most electric, visually arresting things I've ever seen at a festival. Mid-set, Karol G changed into a pair of custom silver Reebok Freestyle Hi Shoes. The Colombian artist recently joined the brand as a global ambassador in time for the relaunch of Reebok’s Classics, so the choice on stage felt like a grounded detail in an otherwise otherworldly show. She splashed around in a pool in them. She danced with surprise guest artist Becky G to “Mamiii” in them. And I looked down at my own Reebok sneakers, feet still intact after a full day, and danced with the rest of the pit. The best Coachella performances make you forget you've been on your feet for ten hours. Karol G's did that. For me, it belongs up there with Beyoncé's 2018 set, the last time I felt a high-energy crowd completely in the performer’s grasp. Near the end, she addressed the crowd in English, a shift from the Spanish she'd spoken throughout: "I am Carolina Giraldo from Medellín, Colombia. And today, I am the first Latina woman to headline Coachella." She paused, then continued, "It feels… late," and dedicated the show to her community. Flags from Colombia, Puerto Rico, Mexico, and El Salvador went up across the crowd. She closed out with the crowd feeling the sentiment of the line she’d opened with: Latina Forever. Jessica (aka Jess) is a Senior Fashion Editor at Cosmopolitan, working across both fashion market and styling for print stories, as well as digital fashion and commerce coverage. Prior to joining Cosmo, she worked in fashion at Vanity Fair. Jess lives in New York City and loves spotlighting emerging designers you might not have heard of yet—while also being an unabashedly devoted Love Island fan (dating back to Season 1 of Love Island UK, that’s how serious). Merit Financial Group LLC increased its holdings in shares of  The Goldman Sachs Group, Inc. title: GS Caltex Setter Ahn Hye-jin Caught Driving Under Influence - 조선일보
content: Club Apologizes, Disciplinary Actions Loom as DUI Threatens Career and National Team Eligibility [OSEN=Hong Jisu Reporter] Professional volleyball player GS Caltex setter Ahn Hye-jin was caught driving under the influence. The GS Caltex volleyball team announced on the 17th, "We have confirmed the fact that player Ahn Hye-jin drove under the influence and inform our fans with a heavy sense of responsibility," adding, "Driving under the influence is an act that cannot be tolerated under any circumstances. The club expresses deep regret for causing concern over this matter and sincerely apologizes to all fans who support our volleyball team and all V-League fans." Ahn Hye-jin was reportedly caught by police driving under the influence in the early morning of the 16th. She led her team to victory in the 2025-2026 season and even obtained FA eligibility, but the DUI incident has caused her to lose the achievements she worked hard to accomplish in an instant. The club immediately notified the Korea Volleyball Association (KOVO) upon confirming the incident. KOVO stated, "Disciplinary actions ranging from a minimum warning and fines to a maximum expulsion are possible," adding, "We plan to finalize the schedule for the disciplinary committee soon." The GS Caltex club stated, "We have requested cooperation to proceed with procedures according to V-League regulations," and added, "We will also thoroughly investigate the circumstances of this incident and take corresponding measures. Furthermore, to prevent recurrence, we will review and improve the education and management systems for players and club staff. We once again deeply apologize to our fans for causing concern and disappointment," conveying the apology through the club's official SNS. Ahn Hye-jin, the winning setter of GS Caltex during the 2025-2026 season, became a free agent (FA) after the 2025-2026 season, but this DUI incident is expected to significantly impact her future career. Her national team eligibility could also be revoked. Ahn Hye-jin was listed in the national team roster announced by the Korea Volleyball Association on the 16th, but individuals who receive a fine of over 5 million Korean won for DUI and have not passed three years since the finalization of their sentence are ineligible to become representative players.

title: All the Shoes That Have Defined Karol G’s Style Over the Years - WWD
content: Karol G is making history with her career, one step at a time. The Colombian superstar became the first Latina headliner at Coachella, she had the highest-grossing tour by a Latina with the “Mañana Será Bonito Tour,” has won one Grammy Award and eight Latin Grammys, all while becoming a force in the fashion industry, carving a spot for Latinas in that space. The singer’s style has expanded as her career has ascended, and she is often spotted in designer footwear that would make any shoe lover envious. She often wears classic brands like Jimmy Choo, Chrisitan Louboutin and Gianvito Rossi, while also making room for more contemporary brands like Amina Muaddi and Sophia Webster. But what the “Tusa” singer loves above all is a clear shoe, a wraparound sandal, and a sky-high heel. The singer has also entered the footwear space, signing on for a partnership with Reebok in January that is set to include a new sneaker collection to arrive in 2027. Until then, take a look at some of Karol G’s most memorable shoe moments and the high heels that have carried her to the top. Karol G performed at the Coachella Stage during the 2026 Coachella Valley Music and Arts Festival at Empire Polo Club on April 12, 2026 in Indio, California. Karol G attends the Schiaparelli Haute Couture fall 2025 show as part of Paris Fashion Week on July 7, 2025 in Paris, France. She wore satin T-strap pumps by Schiaparelli with a crystal spherical detail at the bottom of the heel. Karol G attended Selena Gomez’s 2nd Annual Rare Impact Fund Benefit at nya studios West on Oct. 24, 2024 in Los Angeles, California. She wore burgundy Christian Louboutin 513 Satin Sandals. Karol G won the Billboard Music Awards for Top Latin Female Artist and Top Latin Touring Artist at the 2023 Billboard Music Awards at Line 204 in Los Angeles, California in November 2023. She wore the Gianvito Rossi Metropolis 105 heeled PVC sandals in beige. Karol G performs at the Coachella Stage during the 2026 Coachella Valley Music and Arts Festival at Empire Polo Club on April 12, 2026 in Indio, California. The singer stepped out on stage in a custom silver Reebok Freestyle Hi, reimagining the iconic silhouette to match the bold energy of her set. Her background dancers and band were outfitted in a blend of Reebok shoe styles, including the Club C 85, Workout Plus, Freestyle Lo, Club C Ground UK, and R400. In January 2026, Reebok inked a new multi-year partnership with the singer, reintroducing its Classics collection for a new era. They are set to launch a new collection in 2027. Karol G at the 2025 Victoria’s Secret Fashion Show held at Steiner Studios on Oct. 15, 2025 in New York, New York. She wore the Ferragamo ‘Ghilly’ appliquéed flower satin sandals in red. Karol G at the 2026 Vanity Fair Oscar Party held at the Los Angeles County Museum of Art on March 15, 2026 in Los Angeles, California. She wore her go-to pair of Gianvito Rossi Elle mule sandals with a see-through plexi strap and a pointed toe, which she also wore to the 2026 Grammys. Karol G at the Billboard Women In Music event held at the YouTube Theater on March 2, 2022 in Inglewood, California. She wore Jimmy Choo Saeda 100 crystal-embellished pumps. Karol G at the 23rd Annual Latin Grammy Awards held at the Michelob Ultra Arena on Nov. 17, 2022 in Las Vegas, Nevada. She wrote wrap around, thigh high sandals from the Y/Project fall 2017 collection. Karol G at the 2023 MTV Video Music Awards held at Prudential Center on September 12, 2023 in Newark, New Jersey. She wore Amina Muaddi Dalida Glass PVC platform mules. Karol G at the Pre-Grammy Gala on Jan. 31, 2026 in Los Angeles, California. She wore the René Caovilla Braid Cleo crystal-embellished spiral-wrap sandals in gray. Karol G at the Billboard Latin Music Awards 2023 held at Watsco Center on Oct. 5, 2023 in Coral Gables, Florida. She wore the Sophia Webster Rita 140mm platform sandals in white satin. Karol G at the 2024 MTV Video Music Awards held at UBS Arena on Sept. 11, 2024 in in Elmont, New York. She wore the Amina Muaddi Lupita PVC wedge mules in a bright yellow hue. Karol G at the 2023 MTV Video Music Awards held at Prudential Center on Sept. 12, 2023 in Newark, New Jersey. Her onstage looks are often a bit more toned down than her red carpet moments. Ditching her high heels, the singer wore a pair of the Prada Monolith platform boots in white. Karol G at the Billboard Latin Music Week 2021 event on Sept. 21, 2021 in Miami, Florida. Showing off her go-to clear shoe aesthetic, she wore the Alexander Wang Nova studded slingback sandals, featuring a clear PVC toe strap, a branded slingback, and a silver-tone metal ball chain trim along the sole. Karol G at the Los Angeles premiere of “The Addams Family” on Oct. 6, 2019. She is wearing an oversized light blue blazer paired with a thick black leather belt and dark leggings. The look is completed with knee-high black boots and a small silver handbag. title: GS MODULAR to Showcase MiC Modular Construction Solutions at UK Construction Week London 2026 - 24-7 Press Release Newswire
content:
LONDON, ENGLAND, April 17, 2026 /24-7PressRelease/ -- GS MODULAR, a global modular construction company specializing in Modular Integrated Construction (MiC) and permanent modular building systems, will exhibit at UK Construction Week London 2026, taking place May 12–14, 2026 at ExCeL London in London, United Kingdom. Visitors are invited to meet the GS MODULAR team at Booth H45 to explore the company's latest MiC modular construction technologies.

As the construction industry seeks faster, more efficient, and sustainable building methods, Modular Integrated Construction (MiC) has become an important solution for developers, contractors, and governments worldwide. GS MODULAR advances this approach through integrated modular engineering and factory-based manufacturing capabilities.

Advanced MiC Modular Construction
At UK Construction Week London 2026, GS MODULAR will present its next-generation MiC modular building systems, designed to improve construction efficiency, shorten project timelines, and ensure consistent structural quality.

GS MODULAR's MiC modular construction solutions are widely used in:
·Modular homes
·Modular Hotels
·Commercial Buildings
·Student Apartment
·Educational facilities
·Workforce housing and apartments

Using MiC technology, building modules are completed in a controlled factory environment and then transported to the construction site for rapid installation, enabling faster project delivery while maintaining high standards of quality and safety.

Driving Sustainable Construction
By integrating advanced steel structural systems with MiC technology, GS MODULAR delivers durable, scalable, and environmentally responsible modular buildings that comply with international construction standards.

"Modular Integrated Construction is transforming how buildings are delivered by improving efficiency and reducing construction time," said a GS MODULAR spokesperson.

"GS MODULAR is committed to providing reliable MiC modular building solutions for developers and partners worldwide."

Meet GS MODULAR at Booth H45
Professionals attending UK Construction Week London 2026 are invited to visit Booth H45 to learn more about GS MODULAR's MiC modular construction technology and explore potential collaboration opportunities.

About GS MODULAR
GS MODULAR is a global modular construction company specializing in Modular Integrated Construction (MiC) and permanent modular building systems, delivering high-quality MiC modular buildings for residential, hospitality, education, and commercial projects worldwide.# # #

title: Construction firms hike wages for employees in Middle East - The Korea Times
content: GS E&C employees pose at the construction site for the Fadhili Gas Plant expansion in Saudi Arabia in this undated photo. Courtesy of GS E&C Korean construction firms have begun offering higher pay to employees working at project sites in the Middle East. The wage hikes address persistent safety concerns due to prolonged tensions in the region following the war between the United States, Israel and Iran. Earlier this month, the ceiling of Hyundai E&C's office near the Amiral project site in Saudi Arabia was partially damaged by a tremor caused by an Iranian airstrike on a neighboring petrochemical complex. While some staff have been evacuated, construction firms have offered incentives to employees who remain on duty. Samsung gave gifts worth 5 million won ($3,380) last month to some 500 employees working in the United Arab Emirates, Qatar and Saudi Arabia, most of whom belong to the conglomerate's construction arms, Samsung C&T and Samsung E&A. The gifts included Samsung Electronics mobile devices and vouchers that can be used at traditional markets in Korea. In addition, Samsung E&A decided to raise allowances for employees dispatched to the three Middle Eastern countries classified as dangerous regions. GS E&C took similar measures for employees dispatched to five Middle Eastern countries, while offering days off and hotel vouchers to those returning to Korea. “The company does not take your dedication for granted,” GS E&C CEO Huh Yoon-hong said in a letter sent to employees in the Middle East on March 27. Daewoo E&C is reportedly in talks with its union to increase incentives for employees at Iraqi sites. The company is said to be considering raising allowances and offering days off, department store vouchers and hotel stays. According to the International Contractors Association of Korea, some 220 projects by Korean construction firms are under way in nine Middle Eastern countries. In 2024, orders from the Middle East accounted for 49.8 percent of Korea’s total overseas construction orders. The temporary decline to 25.1 percent last year resulted from a large nuclear plant contract in the Czech Republic. After the outbreak of war, however, construction orders from the Middle East totaled $29.97 million in March, down 88 percent from the previous month. The region’s share of total overseas orders also plunged to 3.7 percent from 56.1 percent during the same period. title: Lexus recalls over 9,100 IS, RC, and GS models over fuel pump defect - Car Dealership Guy News
content: On April 8, the National Highway Traffic Safety Administration (NHTSA) logged a recall filed by Toyota covering 9,139 Lexus vehicles across three models, including the 2022 IS, 2015-2022 RC, and 2016-2020 GS, over a fuel pump defect that could cause a vehicle stall. The issue: According to the NHTSA, a resin impeller inside the low-pressure fuel pump can degrade under certain operating conditions, potentially causing the pump to stop working altogether. When that happens, drivers may see warning lights, experience difficulty starting the vehicle, or face a stall, the last of which carries an elevated crash risk at highway speeds. The fix: Dealers will replace the fuel pump assembly with an improved unit at no charge. No "do not drive" advisory is in place. Dealer notifications went out April 8. Owner letters are expected between May 25 and June 8. For Lexus dealers: The population spans three nameplates and nearly a decade of production dates, so service teams should expect a steady flow of appointments once owner letters land next month. title: Classic 1972 Buick GS Convertible Heads to Spring Carlisle Collector Car Auction - Yahoo Autos
content: A 1972 Buick GS convertible is drawing attention ahead of its appearance at auction, offering collectors a blend of American muscle heritage and open-air driving appeal. Listed as Lot #479 at the Spring Carlisle Collector Car Auction, the vehicle represents a period when performance and style were central to Buick’s Grand Sport lineup. The car is equipped with a 350 cubic-inch V8 engine, a powertrain that reflects the era’s emphasis on balanced performance and drivability. Paired with a four-speed manual transmission, the setup appeals to enthusiasts who value a more hands-on driving experience, reinforcing the vehicle’s identity as a driver-focused classic. As a Grand Sport model, the car carries a performance-oriented badge that distinguished Buick’s muscle offerings during the early 1970s. While the GS nameplate is often associated with higher-displacement engines, the 350 V8 variant still delivered respectable performance and remains a sought-after configuration among collectors seeking a more accessible entry point into vintage muscle cars. The convertible body style adds another layer of desirability. Featuring a power-operated convertible top, the vehicle combines classic styling with convenience, allowing for seamless transition between enclosed comfort and open-air cruising. This feature enhances its appeal for both show and leisure use, particularly in warmer climates or at automotive events. Additional equipment includes power steering and power brakes, both of which contribute to improved drivability and ease of handling. These features reflect a shift during the era toward integrating performance with everyday usability, making classic cars more approachable for a wider audience. The vehicle is being offered in as-is condition, a common designation in collector car auctions that signals buyers to evaluate the car based on its current state. For many enthusiasts, this presents an opportunity to either preserve the car’s originality or undertake a restoration project. With its combination of period-correct performance, classic styling and convertible design, the 1972 Buick GS stands as a notable example of early 1970s American automotive engineering, poised to attract interest from collectors and muscle car enthusiasts alike. title: Local trivia hosts keep format fun and fresh - Toledo Blade
content: Rich Kincaid and Ron Guyor are among the local kings of trivia, but it wasn’t always that way. “Ron and I were big trivia players and name that tune players before we started doing our own thing,” Mr. Kincaid said. “We always had the same complaints when we played trivia ... How much down time there was, how irregular it was. We said man, if we could just do our own, we could do it how we feel it needs to be done.” “We sat down for a couple hours and put our theme together, wrote our script, and it’s taken off from there,” Mr. Kincaid said. Eight years ago, the pair started Rich & Ronnie G’s, a partnership that hosts immersive trivia competitions around the area. The men also work together during the day at MTS Seating in Temperance. With Mr. Kincaid hosting, and Mr. Guyor working the sound boards, building graphics, and managing scoring, they now have four regular gigs around the area and can be hired for private events as well. The pair collaborates on writing the questions, which number over 60 per game. The weekly game at the Stubborn Brother every Tuesday is the most popular with up to 25 teams competing. Attendance is boosted by what is called league play. The year is split into quarters and competitors accumulate points in each game to become seasonal champion. “It draws people to come because if you have a bad night on Tuesday you can come to the Wednesday show to make up some points,” Mr. Guyor said. The Guy in the 419, Pat McCarty, is another local trivia host that writes his own questions. “I try to as much as I can,” he said. “Sometimes you run dry on stuff.” Mr. McCarty has pared down his hosting duties over the last few years but still hosts regular trivia nights in Sylvania Township, Swanton, and Temperance. The content creator says he gets questions from a variety of sources but tries to be as original as possible. “I might look up ‘give me some baseball trivia’ and that will give you some questions from there,” Mr. McCarty said. “Sometimes I say let me dissect this question a little bit then I can come up with several questions from that.” Mr. McCarty’s Tuesday night game at Voodoo Brewing Company brought out a diverse crowd of regular competitors. One table featured three employees of Hawkins STEAM Elementary — Crystal Purdue, Carrie Blum, and Michael Molyet. “We used to go 60cc [Brewing] every other week for trivia but they are under new ownership and are closed,” Mr. Molyet said. Ms. Blum said they tried a few places for a new trivia outlet but settled on Voodoo because of its live host and interesting format. Mr. McCarty’s format involves a sheet of paper with 10 perforated slips. A team can hand in anything from the 10 point slip to the one point slip based on how confident they are in their answer. “We like writing it down better than we like a Sporcle kind of a thing,” Ms. Blum said, citing the popular quiz website many venues base their games on. The group said heading to trivia every week provides a chill space to hang out and something to look forward to. “At the end of every day, I go down to her classroom to pick up the kindergarteners to walk them down to the walker door,” Ms. Blum said gesturing toward Ms. Purdue, a kindergarten teacher. “On Tuesdays, she always looks at me and goes ‘Voodoo?’” One regular in Rich & Ronnie G’s league is Steve Senio, a founding member of the Tropics, a team that includes both friends and family. “I saw it advertised and we signed up because my wife is really into trivia,” Mr. Senio said of his initial foray into Rich & Ronnie G’s games. Mr. Senio said that he has been exposed to a variety of trivia formats in different places like Florida and the Detroit area. “It’s usually four rounds. They don’t use TVs. They don’t have Rich walking around with the microphone. They don’t have buzzers,” Mr. Senio said of other trivia competitions. “Nothing we’ve ever played has come close to being as grandiose and fun and interactive as Rich & Ronnie G’s trivia for sure.” Like many, Mr. Senio praised the formation of community amongst the regulars in the trivia scene. “It’s the camaraderie,” Mr. Senio said. “It’s the fun, it’s the competition.” Mr. McCarty agreed, citing the friendships he has formed with the regulars he sees each week. “It’s gotten to a point that I look forward to seeing them,” he said. Mr. McCarty said he has seen couples go from dating to married during his time moderating and one couple is even bringing their baby now. “I have always said to my wife, there are a couple places that if money wasn’t an issue ... I could win the lottery tomorrow and I would still want to go back out.”

The Goldman Sachs Group, Inc is a global investment banking and financial services firm headquartered in New York City. Founded in 1869 as a commercial paper business, the company has grown into a diversified financial institution that provides a broad range of services to corporations, financial institutions, governments and individuals. The firm is led by Chief Executive Officer David M. Solomon and operates across major financial centers worldwide. Goldman Sachs' core businesses include investment banking, global markets, asset and wealth management, and consumer banking.
